title: Macrame belt
subtitle: A square-knotted cotton cord belt with a D-ring adjustable fastening, approximately 90-110 cm long
excerpt: A macrame belt uses a flat sequence of square knots worked in a narrow band. The cords mount through a D-ring at one end, which serves as both an anchor for the knotting and the adjustable fastening: the finished belt threads back through the ring to adjust the length. This belt uses 4 mm natural cotton or nylon cord and produces a band approximately 2 cm wide and 90-110 cm long before the adjustment loop.
sourceNotes: Macrame belt with D-ring fastening is a documented traditional and contemporary macrame project. Method is observation-led and consistent with current macrame instruction.

content.text: at one end. The ring acts as both the mounting point and the adjustable clasp, removing the need for a separate buckle.

items.name: Cotton or nylon macrame cord, 4 mm
Macrame board, clipboard, or foam block (for anchoring during knotting)

content.text: Step 1: Cut and mount the cords

Cut 4 cords: 2 working cords at 8 m each and 2 filler cords at 2 m each. Fold all 4 cords in half and mount them through the straight bar of the D-ring: push the folded loops through from below and bring the tails over the bar and through the loops. Clip or pin the D-ring to your macrame board or a firm anchor point.
Step 2: Work the square knot band

Separate the 8 working cords into 2 outer cords on each side (the working cords) and 4 inner cords (the fillers). For each square knot: bring the left working cord over the 4 filler cords and under the right working cord; bring the right working cord under the filler cords and up through the loop on the left. Pull both working cords snug. For the second half: bring the right cord over the fillers and under the left; bring the left cord under the fillers and up through. Pull snug. Repeat to build the band.
Step 3: Continue knotting to the required length

Continue working square knots until the band measures approximately 90-110 cm, adjusting for the wearer's waist measurement. The belt threads through the D-ring to fasten, so add 30-40 cm beyond the waist measurement to allow for the fastening overlap. Check the length periodically by holding the band at the waist.

At the finished end of the belt, tie a gathering knot around all 8 cords using a separate 40 cm piece of cord: wind it tightly 6-8 times around the bundle, thread both ends through the coil with a blunt darning needle, pull tight, and trim close. Trim the remaining cord tails to a short fringe of 3-4 cm. To wear, thread the finished end through the D-ring, loop back, and adjust the length by pulling the belt through the ring.

items.fix: A flat square knot requires alternating the knot direction: first half left-over-right, second half right-over-left. If both halves go the same way, the result is a half-hitch spiral.
items.cause: Both halves of the square knot tied in the same direction
items.problem: The band twists into a spiral rather than lying flat

items.fix: For a 100 cm belt, each working cord needs to be at least 500 cm (5 m). If cords run out, splice in new cord by laying it alongside the old and knotting over both for 5-6 knots before trimming the old tail.
items.cause: Working cords cut too short: working cords are consumed at 4-5 times the finished length
items.problem: The working cords run out before the belt is long enough

items.fix: Lay the filler cords flat and parallel before each knot. Pull both working cords with equal tension after each half of the square knot.
items.cause: Filler cords not held parallel, or working cord tension inconsistent
items.problem: The band is uneven in width, wider in some sections than others
